title: Online Guest lecture on Application of Computers in Clinical Settings
content: Event Date: 07-12-2024 The Department of Radiology at the School of Allied Health Sciences, Galgotias University, successfully organized a guest webinar, “Application of Computers in Clinical Settings,” on Saturday, 7th December 2024. The session was delivered by Mr. Aniket Singh, an expert in Computer Science and Engineering, who provided valuable insights into the transformative role of computers in clinical workflows. Attendees explored topics such as patient record management, diagnostic tools, imaging technologies, telemedicine, and hospital information systems, emphasizing the integration of technology into modern healthcare practices. The event, held online on Zoom, was convened by Mr. Saket Srivastav, and the speaker was warmly welcomed by the Program Chair, Mr. Amit Pratap Singh Chouhan. The session was a tremendous success, fostering knowledge exchange and encouraging interdisciplinary collaboration. It aligned with multiple Sustainable Development Goals, including SDG 3 (Good Health and Well-Being), SDG 4 (Quality Education), SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation, and Infrastructure), and SDG 17 (Partnerships for the Goals). During the webinar, Mr. Aniket Singh delivered an engaging and insightful presentation, sharing practical applications of AI-driven diagnostics, predictive analytics, and robotic assistance in surgeries. His presentation highlighted the role of innovation in improving healthcare services and inspired attendees to think beyond traditional approaches. The session concluded with an interactive Q&A segment, where students and faculty actively participated, seeking deeper insights and gaining practical advice from the speaker. The enthusiastic engagement during the session demonstrated the attendees’ keen interest and the webinar’s success in sparking intellectual curiosity. In conclusion, the guest webinar by Mr. Aniket Singh was a remarkable success. It not only inspired students and faculty but also strengthened the university’s commitment to fostering innovation, interdisciplinary collaboration, and sustainable development. The Department looks forward to hosting more such events to continue enriching the academic and professional growth of its community.
